name: CodeMux for Zed - Complete remaining user stories

description: Baseline: Binary size 2.0M. All 71 tests passing, clippy clean, fmt clean. Starting work on remaining user stories.

Added Zed extension structure (extension.toml, Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs), GitHub release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml), and updated authors email. All 71 tests passing, clippy clean. codemux --version correctly shows 0.1.0.

asi.hypothesis: Adding Zed extension structure and release workflow for US-001 and US-003
asi.completed_items: Zed extension directory with proper manifest; GitHub release workflow for multi-platform binaries; Authors email updated; Version flag working
asi.next_steps: Verify all US stories complete; Document extension submission process; Final verification

description: COMPLETED: All 8 user stories verified. Created US_VERIFICATION.md documenting all acceptance criteria. Extension structure complete (extension.toml, Cargo.toml with cdylib, src/lib.rs). Release workflow ready for multi-platform binaries. All 71 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

asi.hypothesis: Completing all remaining user stories from PRD
asi.summary: All 8 US stories completed with 100% acceptance criteria coverage
asi.deliverables: US_VERIFICATION.md, extension/ directory, .github/workflows/release.yml, updated Cargo.toml with authors email
asi.v1.1_deferred: Homebrew tap formula (as per PRD roadmap)
asi.manual_steps: User needs to: 1) Push tag to trigger release workflow, 2) Submit PR to zed-industries/extensions

description: Removed regex dependency by replacing regex-based sanitization with manual string operations. Binary size reduced from 2048 KB to 916 KB (55% reduction). All 71 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing regex dependency with manual string operations will significantly reduce binary size while maintaining correctness
asi.approach: Replaced 4 regex patterns with manual char iteration: invalid char replacement, dash collapsing, leading/trailing dash stripping

description: Added panic = "abort" to release profile. Binary size reduced from 916 KB to 784 KB (additional 14% reduction, 132 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 62% (2048 KB → 784 KB). All 71 tests pass.

asi.hypothesis: panic = "abort" removes unwinding code and reduces binary size
asi.trade_off: Removes panic stack traces - acceptable for CLI tool that execs into multiplexer

description: Added codegen-units = 1 to release profile for better LTO. Binary size reduced from 784 KB to 736 KB (additional 6% reduction, 48 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 64% (2048 KB → 736 KB). Compile time increased slightly (9.9s → 13.1s). All 71 tests pass.

asi.hypothesis: codegen-units = 1 enables better LTO cross-crate optimization
asi.trade_off: Slightly slower compile time (9.9s → 13.1s) - acceptable for release builds

description: Optimized clap features by using default-features = false with minimal feature set (derive, std, help, usage). Binary size reduced from 736 KB to 704 KB (additional 4% reduction, 32 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 66% (2048 KB → 704 KB). All 71 tests pass, --version and --help work correctly.

asi.hypothesis: Using minimal clap features reduces binary size while maintaining core CLI functionality
asi.functionality_verified: --version works, --help works with proper usage line, all 71 tests pass

description: Replaced `which` crate with manual PATH lookup implementation. Binary size remains 704 KB (which was already optimized out by LTO). All 71 tests pass. Removed one external dependency, simplifying the dependency tree and reducing future maintenance burden.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing which crate with manual PATH lookup reduces binary size and dependencies
asi.benefit: Simpler dependency tree, less external code to trust, faster compile times after clean
asi.implementation: Added find_in_path() function using std::env::var("PATH") and Path::is_file()

description: Replaced `dirs` crate with manual platform-specific config directory lookup. Binary size remains 704 KB (dirs was already fully LTO-optimized). All 71 tests pass. Simplified dependency tree further - removed two deps (dirs and dirs-sys).

asi.hypothesis: Replacing dirs crate with manual platform config dir lookup reduces binary size and dependencies
asi.benefit: Simpler dependency tree, faster compile times, less external code
asi.implementation: Added platform_config_dir() with cfg macros for macOS, Linux, Windows, and Unix fallback

description: Replaced clap with manual argument parsing. Binary size reduced from 704 KB to 540 KB (additional 23% reduction, 164 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 74% (2048 KB → 540 KB). All 71 tests pass, --version and --help work correctly.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing clap with manual argument parsing significantly reduces binary size
asi.implementation: Added parse_args() function with 20 lines of manual arg parsing

description: Replaced anyhow with std::io::Error. Binary size reduced from 540 KB to 524 KB (additional 3% reduction, 16 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 74% (2048 KB → 524 KB). All 71 tests pass, --version and --help work correctly.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing anyhow with std::io::Error reduces binary size
asi.implementation: Changed all Result types from anyhow::Result to std::io::Result, replaced anyhow::anyhow! with io::Error::new
asi.trade_off: Slightly more verbose error creation, but no loss in functionality

description: Replaced serde+toml with manual config parser. Binary size reduced from 524 KB to 380 KB (additional 27% reduction, 144 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 81% (2048 KB → 380 KB). All 75 tests pass (added 4 new config parsing tests), --version and --help work correctly. Compile time dramatically reduced from 15s to 4s.

asi.hypothesis: Replacing serde+toml with manual config parser significantly reduces binary size and compile time
asi.dependencies_removed: serde v1.0, toml v0.8, indexmap, hashbrown, and 6 other transitive deps
asi.implementation: Added 50-line manual TOML parser in parse_config_str() supporting multiplexer="value" and auto_attach=true/false
asi.test_coverage: Added 4 new tests for config parsing edge cases

description: Changed opt-level from 3 to "z" (size optimization). Binary size reduced from 380 KB to 348 KB (additional 8% reduction, 32 KB saved). Total reduction from baseline: 83% (2048 KB → 348 KB). All 75 tests pass, --version and --help work correctly. Compile time further reduced to 3.5s.

asi.hypothesis: Using opt-level=z (size optimization) instead of opt-level=3 reduces binary size
asi.trade_off: Potentially slightly slower runtime - acceptable for CLI tool that execs into multiplexer

description: Changed opt-level from "z" to "s" with overflow-checks=false. Binary size remains 348 KB (no change). opt-level "s" and "z" produce identical results for this codebase. overflow-checks=false had no effect (code has minimal arithmetic). All 75 tests pass.

asi.hypothesis: opt-level=s with overflow-checks=false might produce smaller or equivalent binary with better runtime performance
asi.opt_level_comparison: z vs s = identical size for this codebase
asi.overflow_checks: No reduction - codebase has minimal arithmetic operations
asi.insight: 348 KB appears to be near-optimal for current feature set
asi.remaining_opportunities: Linker-level optimizations (estimated 5-15 KB), UPX compression (post-build), or code feature removal

description: Fixed review feedback: (1) Windows binary extension handling now checks both 'binary' and 'binary.exe' without creating double extensions, (2) macOS config path now uses ~/.config/ matching XDG standard and documentation, (3) Added trailing newline to extension.toml per POSIX convention, (4) Enhanced documentation for TOML parser limitations. All 75 tests pass, binary size unchanged at 348 KB.

asi.windows_extension_fix: Check binary.exe only if binary doesn't already end with .exe
asi.toml_docs: Added limitations documentation for manual parser

description: RESEARCH COMPLETE: Tested additional linker-level optimizations (-no_compact_unwind, dead_strip variants). No further size reduction achieved - 348 KB appears to be the optimal size for this codebase on macOS with current settings. All 75 tests pass, full functionality preserved. Binary is 83% smaller than baseline with zero external dependencies.

asi.conclusion: Reached optimal size with current feature set and standard Rust toolchain optimizations on macOS
asi.remaining_opportunities: UPX compression (post-build), musl target for Linux, or feature removal

description: Consolidated shell_escape module into main.rs (removed src/shell_escape.rs, inlined function and tests). Binary size unchanged at 348 KB - LTO was already optimizing across modules. All 75 tests pass. Module count reduced from 7 to 6, slightly simpler project structure.

asi.hypothesis: Consolidating small modules might reduce binary size by helping LTO
asi.conclusion: LTO was already fully optimizing across module boundaries - no size change
asi.benefit: Simpler project structure with fewer files

description: Consolidated launcher module into main.rs (removed src/launcher.rs, inlined MuxLauncher trait). Binary size unchanged at 348 KB. All 75 tests pass. Modules reduced from 6 to 5. LTO continues to fully optimize across crate boundaries.

asi.hypothesis: Further module consolidation might reveal size savings
asi.conclusion: LTO fully optimizes across all module boundaries - further consolidation yields no benefit
asi.remaining_modules: config, detect, sanitize, tmux, zellij (5 total)

description: FINAL STATE: Reverted debug removal attempt - minimal code size reduction for significant functionality loss. Binary remains at optimal 348 KB. All 75 tests pass. Zero external dependencies. 83% size reduction from baseline achieved through: regex→manual, clap→manual args, serde+toml→manual parser, anyhow→std::io::Error, which+dirs→manual, panic=abort, codegen-units=1, opt-level=s, LTO optimizations.

asi.research_status: OPTIMAL - NO FURTHER SIGNIFICANT REDUCTIONS POSSIBLE
asi.remaining_opportunities: Feature removal (debug/config) would sacrifice functionality for minimal (<5KB) gain
asi.conclusion: 348 KB is optimal for current feature set with standard Rust toolchain

description: Added #[inline] hints to hot functions (sanitize_session_name, get_unique_session_name, shell_escape). Binary size unchanged at 348 KB - LTO with codegen-units=1 already performs optimal inlining. All 75 tests pass.

asi.hypothesis: Adding #[inline] hints might help compiler optimize small hot functions
asi.conclusion: LTO + codegen-units=1 already performs optimal inlining decisions - manual hints provide no benefit
asi.benefit: Documentation of hot functions for future maintainers
